evidence.scientific_clarity: All four panels (pipeline diagram, ablation bars, training curve, heatmap) clearly communicate the brief's claims; panel A reads as a scientific diagram, not decoration.
evidence.typography: Script explicitly sets font.sans-serif to ['DejaVu Sans'] (the matplotlib default) and does not set pdf.fonttype=42; PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap fonts instead of Type 42 (TrueType), making text non-selectable/non-editable in the PDF.
evidence.palette: Baseline/Ours use gray/orange (CB-safe pair); ablation uses blue/gray (CB-safe); however panel D uses RdYlGn colormap which is not colorblind-safe (red-green confusion) and uses directional red/green for a non-directional sensitivity score.
evidence.layout_density: gridspec(2,3,width_ratios=[2,1,1]) with full-width bottom panel matches brief exactly; panels fill space well, no gaping whitespace; value labels in panel B are tight but readable.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content accurately and includes the specific numerical claim (64.7%, Δ=9.6 pp); legend in panel C is inside the axes (acceptable); panel D lacks axis title disambiguation—'Hyperparameter S' vs. 'P' could confuse without the caption.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 (bitmap) fonts, not Type 42 (TrueType); text is not selectable in the PDF; script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42 or svg.fonttype=None.
evidence.file_format: All four required files exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf (50 KB), figure.png (183 KB), figure.svg (111 KB), caption.tex (580 B), gen_figure.py (>200 lines).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains each panel with scientific detail; includes specific numerical findings (64.7% full-model accuracy, Δ=9.6 pp memory contribution, 30k training steps, 5×5 grid); bold panel labels match figure.

overall_strengths: Complete 4-panel layout matches the brief exactly (gridspec 2,3 with width_ratios [2,1,1] and full-width bottom panel), all panels are scientifically informative.
Caption is publication-quality: covers all panels, includes specific numerical claims, and uses proper LaTeX formatting with bold panel references.
overall_weaknesses: Critical typography failure: pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, resulting in Type 3 bitmap fonts in the PDF rather than selectable/editable TrueType text—a common journal submission rejection reason.

Panel D uses the RdYlGn colormap which is not colorblind-safe (red-green deficiency affects ~8% of males); a perceptually-uniform diverging or sequential colormap (e.g., viridis, cividis, RdBu) should be used instead.
